steps.title: Define typed locator and shared authority contracts
steps.goal: Create venue-neutral contracts for entity identity, locators, ambiguity sets, no-claim outcomes, evaluator evidence, and spend thresholds.
exitCriteria: At least one new shared authority contract module exists.
One live query surface consumes the new contract directly.
The contract names do not freeze legacy projection identity into the public center of gravity.

steps.title: Move query.navigate onto shared authority contracts
steps.goal: Keep moving navigation logic off direct legacy projection stitching and onto shared authority and evaluator surfaces.
exitCriteria: Host-side query.navigate always enters through a named authority surface.
Package, type, export, file, and symbol navigation no longer rely on ad hoc direct projection reads for their primary resolution path.
The transitional adapter shrinks instead of accreting new projection-shaped reasoning.

steps.title: Move route-witness and audit onto shared authority and evaluators
steps.goal: Move route-witness and then audit off raw legacy projection carriers so they spend the same shared authority and evaluator seams already used by query.navigate.
exitCriteria: route-witness primary route and freshness classification paths no longer assemble truth directly from analysis.deps, analysis.typeRefs, and analysis.exports.
audit reuses the same shared authority or evaluator seams for localization, boundary classification, and route evidence instead of ad hoc query-local joins.
The work does not promote deps, typerefs, or exports back into first-class architectural peers.

steps.title: Replace natural-language and heuristic API paths with machine-legible primitives
steps.goal: Remove the current conversational ingress shell from the primary package API and replace it with typed semantic primitives that expose one truth core through durable answer algebra, result kinds, governing-anchor refs, provenance, closure basis, and continuation basis.
exitCriteria: The primary machine-facing API no longer requires natural-language question strings to reach semantic work.
Semantic work is reachable through typed primitives and selectors rather than capability or inquiry family ranking.
Noun/verb/alias/confusion matching is gone from the primary machine-facing path.
Structured answer algebra, result kinds, governing anchors, provenance, closure basis, and continuation basis remain available without the conversational shell.

steps.title: Split the overloaded inquiry ontology into narrower families
steps.goal: Separate route families, read modes, provenance families, and execution posture so broad inquiry unions remain only at the outer boundary where they are genuinely needed.
exitCriteria: High-fanout internal call sites no longer need the broad FocusKind or world/provenance unions when they only support a narrower family.
Policy, catalog, and answer-envelope code mostly speak narrower route, read-mode, and provenance families.
The refactor avoids freezing premature public names for ontology slices that still need operator steering.

steps.title: Deepen symbol, export, call, and flow semantics
Land Aurelia-aware semantics on top of shared primitives
